patches.find: The claimed prospective prediction cites r = 0.96 while the same-day data shows r = 0.91.
patches.replace: The claimed prospective prediction cites r = 0.96 while Exhibit A on the same home page shows r = 0.992 for the identical storm dataset — two r-values that cannot both be accurate statistics of the same data.
patches.rationale: TLDR says r = 0.91 but detail_evidence says r = 0.992. The r = 0.91 is a phantom value appearing nowhere in the detailed text. Fix to match detail_evidence and clarify the inconsistency.

patches.find: The coupling constant κ = 1.672 is not derived from dome geometry
patches.replace: The coupling constant κ = 1.677 (dome rounds to 1.67) is not derived from dome geometry
patches.rationale: kappa = 1.672 is a transcription error. Actual computation 10.9/6.5 = 1.6769 rounds to 1.677. Dome uses 1.67 (2dp). Correcting to 1.677.

patches.find: Triple-counts WIN-010/WIN-025; Chapman Sq mechanism (1933) explains all; dome provides no magnitude derivation
patches.replace: Dome's sun-altitude refraction fix makes eclipse shadow geometry indeterminate (Section 1.8 cascade); also triple-counts WIN-010/025 and relabels Chapman (1933) ionospheric physics
patches.rationale: Finding field describes Std Model Explains argument but verdict is Self-Contradicted. Add cascade language as primary argument.

patches.find: Kim & Chang (2018) documented real geomagnetic variations during 39 eclipses. But the eclipse-induced magnetic effect has been explained by ionospheric conductivity reduction since Chapman (1933). The dome cites a real paper but relabels a 93-year-old standard explanation.
patches.replace: The dome's own refraction fix splits the sun into apparent altitude (5,733 km) and physical altitude (1,975–3,618 km by season), making the physical sun position indeterminate. Eclipse shadow geometry requires a definite physical sun position, so the dome cannot specify where the physical sun was for any of the 39 eclipses in its ensemble. Additionally, eclipse geomagnetic effects have been explained by ionospheric conductivity reduction (Chapman 1933) for 93 years.
patches.rationale: TLDR describes Std Model Explains. Must lead with Self-Contradicted cascade argument. Chapman point preserved as secondary.

patches.find: Eclipse geomagnetic effects have been standard physics since 1933 — 93 years before the dome claimed them. The dome relabels Chapman's mechanism without adding new physics.
patches.replace: The dome's own refraction fix (Section 1.8) makes the physical sun altitude seasonally indeterminate (1,975–3,618 km), so the dome cannot specify the eclipse shadow geometry it claims to predict. The claim is self-contradicted by the dome's own architecture. Additionally, the geomagnetic effect has been standard ionospheric physics since Chapman (1933).
patches.rationale: TLDR verdict describes Std Model Explains argument. Rewrite to lead with Self-Contradicted cascade argument.
